title: RAG Anti-Patterns
compiled_truth:
RAG anti patterns are the dominant failure mode in production retrieval-augmented generation systems today. This talk surveys the eight most common RAG anti patterns we see in production deployments and the structural fixes for each.

The canonical RAG anti patterns: (1) chunk-first thinking ... building the retriever before knowing what gets asked. (2) cosine-only ranking ... assuming vector similarity is enough. (3) reranking-as-cherry-on-top ... doing reranking but not measuring whether it helps. (4) ignoring intent ... treating temporal queries the same as entity queries. (5) the swamp problem ... bulky low-signal pages crowd out curated content because keyword density is higher per byte. (6) source-blind ranking ... treating a chat dump and an essay as equally authoritative. (7) over-retrieval ... fetching 50 chunks and praying the LLM can sort them. (8) under-retrieval ... fetching 3 and missing the answer.

The talk closes with the structural fix for each ... most are a few lines of SQL or a small reranking pass. None require a model upgrade.
timeline: - **2026-04-19** | Talk delivered. Got asked about gbrain's source-boost work.
